legacyOptions.expr: sum(vm_app_version{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(version) unless (sum(vm_app_version{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"} offset $__interval) by(version))

spec.description: Overview for single-node VictoriaMetrics v1.117.0 or higher

spec.title: Datapoints ingestion rate ($instance)
spec.description: How many datapoints are inserted into storage per second

spec.title: Datapoints read per series
spec.description: 99th percentile of number of [data samples](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/keyconcepts/#raw-samples) read per queried series.

spec.title: Datapoints scanned per query
spec.description:
99th percentile of number of [data samples](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/keyconcepts/#raw-samples) scanner per query.

This number can exceed number of RowsReadPerQuery if `step` query arg passed to [/api/v1/query_range](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/querying/api/#range-queries) is smaller than the lookbehind window set in square brackets of [rollup function](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/metricsql/#rollup-functions). For example, if `increase(some_metric[1h])` is executed with the `step=5m`, then the same [data samples](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/keyconcepts/#raw-samples) on a hour time range are scanned `1h/5m=12` times. See [this article](https://valyala.medium.com/how-to-optimize-promql-and-metricsql-queries-85a1b75bf986) for details.

spec.title: Datapoints ingestion rate
spec.description: How many datapoints are inserted into storage per second

spec.title: Ingestion rate
spec.description: Shows the datapoints ingestion rate.

spec.title: Read requests
spec.description: Shows the rate of HTTP read requests.

spec.title: Logging rate
spec.description: Shows the rate of logging the messages by their level. Unexpected spike in rate is a good reason to check logs.

spec.title: RSS memory % usage ($instance)
spec.description:
How much resident memory (RAM) the process is actually using, compared to its allowed container or system limit.

- Good: Below 70% most of the time, maybe spiking a bit under load.
- Bad: Above 90% for more than 5 minutes = risk of out-of-memory (OOM) kill.

spec.title: Assisted merges
spec.description: Merge assist happens when storage can't keep up with merging parts. This is usually a sign of overload for storage.

spec.title: Cache miss ratio ($instance)
spec.description:
Shows cache miss ratio.

**Lower is better.**

spec.expr:
max(
    rate(vm_cache_misses_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])
    /
    rate(vm_cache_requests_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])
) by (type)

spec.title: Requests rate ($instance)
spec.description:
* `*` - unsupported query path
* `/write` - insert into VM
* `/metrics` - query VM system metrics
* `/query` - query instant values
* `/query_range` - query over a range of time
* `/series` - match a certain label set
* `/label/{}/values` - query a list of label values (variables mostly)

spec.title: Datapoints read per query
spec.description: 99th percentile of number of [data samples](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/keyconcepts/#raw-samples) read per query.

spec.title: RSS anonymous memory % usage ($instance)
spec.description:
Share for memory allocated by the process itself. When memory usage reaches 100% it will be likely OOM-killed.
Safe memory usage % considered to be below 80%

spec.title: Disk write/read calls ($instance)
spec.description: Shows the number of read/write syscalls such as read, pread, write, pwrite.

spec.title: CPU spent on GC ($instance)
spec.description:
Shows the percent of CPU spent on garbage collection.

If % is high, then CPU usage can be decreased by changing GOGC to higher values. Increasing GOGC value will increase memory usage, and decrease CPU usage.

Try searching for keyword `GOGC` at https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/troubleshooting/

spec.title: Non-default flags
spec.description: Flags explicitly set to non-default values

spec.expr: sum(flag{is_set="true", job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance, name, value)

spec.title: Network usage
spec.description:
Shows network usage by VM:
* Writes show traffic sent to clients
* Reads show traffic received from clients

spec.title: Go scheduling latency
spec.description:
Shows the time goroutines have spent in runnable state before actually running.

**Lower is better.**

High values or values exceeding the threshold is usually a sign of            insufficient CPU resources or CPU throttling. 

Verify that service has enough CPU resources. Otherwise, the service could work unreliably with delays in processing.

spec.title: Restarts ($job)
spec.description: Shows the number of restarts per job. The chart can be useful to identify periodic process restarts and correlate them with potential issues or anomalies. Normally, processes shouldn't restart unless restart was inited by user. The reason of restarts should be figured out by checking the logs of each specific service.

spec.title: Number of snapshots
spec.description:
Shows number of existing [snapshots](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/single-server-victoriametrics/#how-to-work-with-snapshots).

Recently made snapshots do not occupy disk space. But with time, as snapshots become older, they start to occupy more and more disk space.

It is recommended deleting old snapshots when they are no longer needed to free up disk space.

spec.title: Samples dropped for last 1h
Deduplication rate ($instance)
spec.description:
Shows rate of deduplicated samples during [deduplication](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/single-server-victoriametrics/#deduplication) or [downsampling](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/single-server-victoriametrics/#downsampling), according to the configured `-dedup.minScrapeInterval` or `-downsampling.period`.

Should be empty if `-dedup.minScrapeInterval` and `-downsampling.period` isn't set.

Deduplication happens in two places:
* `type="merge"` - during [background merges](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/single-server-victoriametrics/#storage). Deduplication during merges is permanent.
* `type="select"` - during [read queries](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/keyconcepts/#query-data). Deduplication happens in-flight and has no permanent effect.

The more samples need to be deduplicated, the higher will be resource usage.

spec.title: Memory allocations rate
spec.description: Shows the rate of allocations in memory. Sudden increase in allocations would mean increased pressure on Go Garbage Collector and can saturate CPU resources of the application.

spec.title: CPU pressure
spec.description:
CPU pressure based on [Pressure Stall Information](https://docs.kernel.org/accounting/psi.html).

**Lower is better.**

Pressure is measured as amount of time within 1sec time window the process was:
- waiting: at least one task in the process was ready to run (runnable) but couldn't get scheduled on the CPU.
- stalled: all tasks in the process (except idle ones) were unable to get CPU time — a full CPU stall.

If there's a CPU burst, it's normal to see waiting or stalled > 100ms. It only becomes a concern if it consistently climbs above 50-100ms and aligns with latency spikes or GC slowdowns.

spec.title: Memory pressure
spec.description:
Shows memory pressure based on [Pressure Stall Information](https://docs.kernel.org/accounting/psi.html).

**Lower is better.**

Pressure is measured as amount of time within 1sec time window the process was:
- waiting: at least one thread was blocked on memory.
- stalled: every thread was blocked on memory (severe pressure).

Elevated memory pressure can slowdown the process performance by utilizing more disk IO. Consider increasing amount of available RAM limit or decreasing the load on the process.

Seу major page faults rate panel in Troubleshooting section if this metric continued to be high.

spec.title: IO pressure
spec.description:
Shows IO pressure based on [Pressure Stall Information](https://docs.kernel.org/accounting/psi.html).

**Lower is better.**

Pressure is measured as amount of time within 1sec time window the process was:
- waiting: at least one runnable thread blocked on IO (disk, NVMe, network-storage) while others could still make progress.
- stalled: all non-idle threads simultaneously waiting on `I/O`.

If stalled > 0, consider increasing queue depth on NVMe, raising blk-mq budgets, or relaxing cgroup I/O limits.

spec.title: Major page faults rate
spec.description:
Major page faults occur when required data is not in physical memory and must be loaded from disk (storage or swap). It usually happens with:
- insufficient physical memory
- active use of swap space
- file mapping access

It may cause significant performance slowdown if sustained high.

Possible workaround:
- Extend RAM to scale up
- Reduce swap usage by adjusting vm.swappiness

options.content: Drilldown row is used by other panels on the dashboard to show more detailed metrics per-instance, or a single instance in cases like memory breakdown.

spec.title: Major page faults rate
spec.description:
Major page faults occur when required data is not in physical memory and must be loaded from disk (storage or swap). It usually happens with:
- insufficient physical memory
- active use of swap space
- file mapping access

It may cause significant performance slowdown if sustained high.

Possible workaround:
- Extend RAM to scale up
- Reduce swap usage by adjusting vm.swappiness

spec.title: Memory usage breakdown
spec.description:
This panel breaks down memory usage by type. It’s intended for inspecting a single instance.

How to use:
- Start from the high-level RSS panel.
- Identify an instance with unexpected or abnormal memory growth.
- Filter to that instance to inspect the detailed breakdown.

Interpretation
- A steadily rising Go Heap usually indicates a memory leak. Collect pprof memory profile.
- A growing Go Stack commonly points to a goroutine leak.

spec.expr:
# go other
max(
    go_memstats_gc_sys_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"} +
    go_memstats_mspan_sys_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"} +
    go_memstats_other_sys_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"} +
    go_memstats_mcache_sys_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}
)

# vm cache
max(sum(vm_cache_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by (job, instance))

spec.title: Open FDs usage % ($instance)
spec.description:
Shows the percentage of open file descriptors compared to the limit set in the OS.
Reaching the limit of open files can cause various issues and must be prevented.

See how to change limits here https://medium.com/@muhammadtriwibowo/set-permanently-ulimit-n-open-files-in-ubuntu-4d61064429a

spec.title: Concurrent flushes on disk ($instance)
spec.description:
Shows how many ongoing insertions (not API /write calls) on disk are taking place, where:
* `max` - equal to number of CPUs;
* `current` - current number of goroutines busy with inserting rows into underlying storage.

Every successful API /write call results into flush on disk. The `max` is an internal limit and can't be changed. It is always equal to the number of CPUs. 

When `current` hits `max` constantly, it means storage is overloaded and requires more CPU (see CPU usage) or disks with more IOPS (see disk writes and reads panels in Resource Usage row).

spec.title: Disk space usage %  ($instance)
spec.description: Shows the percentage of used disk space. It is recommended to have at least 20% of free disk space for the best performance.

spec.expr:
max(
    sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance) /
    (
        sum(vm_free_disk_space_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}-vm_free_disk_space_limit_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance) +
        sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance)
    ) 
)

min(
    sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance) /
    (
        sum(vm_free_disk_space_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}-vm_free_disk_space_limit_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance) +
        sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance)
    ) 
)

quantile(0.5,
    sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance) /
    (
        sum(vm_free_disk_space_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}-vm_free_disk_space_limit_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance) +
        sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance)
    ) 
)

spec.title: Disk space usage % by type  ($instance)
spec.description:
Shows the percentage of used disk space by type: datapoints or indexdb. Normally, indexdb takes much less space comparing to datapoints. But with high [churn rate](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/faq/#what-is-high-churn-rate) the size of the indexdb could grow significantly.

The sum of the % can be > 100% since panel shows max % per-job and per-instance. It means different instance can have different ratio between datapoints and indexdb size.

spec.expr:
max(
    sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type=~"indexdb.*"}) by(job, instance)
    / 
    sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance)
)

max(
    sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type!~"indexdb.*"}) by(job, instance)
    / 
    sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by(job, instance)
)

spec.title: Pending datapoints ($instance)
spec.description: How many data points are in RAM queue waiting to be written into storage. The number of pending data points should be in the range from 0 to `5*<ingestion_rate>`, since VictoriaMetrics pushes pending data to persistent storage every 5 seconds. The index datapoints value in general is much lower.

spec.title: RSS memory usage ($instance)
spec.description:
Shows the used memory (resident).
The application's performance will significantly degrade when memory usage is close to 100%.

spec.title: RSS anonymous memory usage ($instance)
spec.description: RSS share for memory allocated by the process itself. This share cannot be freed by the OS, so it must be taken into account by OOM killer.

spec.title: Storage full ETA ($instance)

spec.expr:
(vm_free_disk_space_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}-vm_free_disk_space_limit_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) 
/ 
ignoring(path) (
    (rate(vm_rows_added_to_storage_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[1d]) - 
        sum(rate(vm_deduplicated_samples_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[1d])) without(type)) * 
    (
        sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type!~"indexdb.*"}) without(type) /
        sum(vm_rows{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type!~"indexdb.*"}) without(type)
    )
    +
    rate(vm_new_timeseries_created_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[1d]) * 
    (
        sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type="indexdb/file"}) without(type) /
        sum(vm_rows{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type="indexdb/file"}) without(type)
    )
) > 0

spec.title: Disk space usage ($instance)
spec.description: Shows the percentage of used disk space. It is recommended to have at least 20% of free disk space for the best performance.

spec.title: Logging rate
spec.description: Shows the rate of logging the messages by their level. Unexpected spike in rate is a good reason to check logs.

spec.expr: sum(rate(vm_log_messages_total{job=~"$job",instance=~"$instance", level!="info"}[$__rate_interval])) by (job, instance, level, app_version, location) > 0

sum by(job, version) (label_replace(vm_app_version{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", short_version!=""}, "version", "$1", "short_version", "(.*)") or vm_app_version{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", short_version=""})

spec.title: Query duration 0.99 quantile ($instance)
spec.description:
The less time it takes is better.
* `*` - unsupported query path
* `/write` - insert into VM
* `/metrics` - query VM system metrics
* `/query` - query instant values
* `/query_range` - query over a range of time
* `/series` - match a certain label set
* `/label/{}/values` - query a list of label values (variables mostly)

spec.title: Total datapoints
spec.description: How many datapoints are in storage

spec.title: Datapoints
spec.description: Shows how many datapoints are in the storage and what is average disk usage per datapoint.

spec.expr:
sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by (job)
/ sum(vm_rows{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type!~"indexdb.*"}) by (job)

spec.title: Pending datapoints
spec.description: How many data points are in RAM queue waiting to be written into storage. The number of pending data points should be in the range from 0 to `5*<ingestion_rate>`, since VictoriaMetrics pushes pending data to persistent storage every 5 seconds. The index datapoints value in general is much lower

spec.title: Requests error rate ($instance)
spec.description:
* `*` - unsupported query path
* `/write` - insert into VM
* `/metrics` - query VM system metrics
* `/query` - query instant values
* `/query_range` - query over a range of time
* `/series` - match a certain label set
* `/label/{}/values` - query a list of label values (variables mostly)

spec.title: LSM parts max by type ($instance)
spec.description:
The max number of data parts of LSM tree across all storage nodes in the last [partition](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/single-server-victoriametrics/#storage).
Increase in number of parts (the hard limit is 512) is an evidence of slow merge performance - check the resource utilization.
* `indexdb` - inverted index
* `storage/small` - recently added parts of data ingested into storage (hot data)
* `storage/big` -  small parts gradually merged into bigger parts (cold data)

spec.title: Active series
spec.description: Shows the number of [active time series](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/faq/#what-is-an-active-time-series) with new data points inserted during the last hour. High value may result in ingestion slowdown.

spec.expr: sum(go_memstats_sys_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by (job) + sum(vm_cache_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) by (job)

spec.title: Active time series
spec.description:
Shows the number of [active time series](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/faq/#what-is-an-active-time-series) with new data points inserted during the last hour. High value may result in ingestion slowdown. 

See following link for details:

spec.title: Disk space usage - datapoints
spec.description: Shows amount of on-disk space occupied by data points and the remaining disk space at `-storageDataPath`. Calculation accounts for -storage.minFreeDiskSpaceBytes

spec.title: CPU
spec.description: CPU cores used by instance

spec.title: Rows ignored for last 1h
spec.description: Shows how many rows were ignored on insertion due to corrupted or out of retention timestamps and how many series were ignored on insertion or during scrape due to too long label names, values and excessive labels count.

spec.title: Concurrent flushes on disk {$instance}
spec.description:
Shows how many ongoing insertions (not API /write calls) on disk are taking place, where:
* `max` - equal to number of CPUs;
* `current` - current number of goroutines busy with inserting rows into underlying storage.

Every successful API /write call results into flush on disk. However, these two actions are separated and controlled via different concurrency limiters. The `max` on this panel can't be changed and always equal to number of CPUs. 

When `current` hits `max` constantly, it means storage is overloaded and requires more CPU.

spec.title: Slow queries rate
spec.description: Slow queries rate according to `search.logSlowQueryDuration` flag, which is `5s` by default.

spec.title: Active merges ($instance)
spec.description: The number of on-going merges in storage nodes.  It is expected to have high numbers for `storage/small` metric.

spec.title: Merge speed
spec.description: The number of rows merged per second by storage nodes.

spec.title: Churn rate ($instance)
spec.description:
Shows the rate and total number of new series created over last 24h.

High [churn rate](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/faq/#what-is-high-churn-rate) tightly connected with database performance and may result in unexpected OOM's or slow queries. It is recommended to always keep an eye on this metric to avoid unexpected [cardinality](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/keyconcepts/#cardinality) "explosions".

The higher churn rate is, the more resources required to handle it. Consider to keep the churn rate as low as possible.

Good references to read:
* https://www.robustperception.io/cardinality-is-key
* https://www.robustperception.io/using-tsdb-analyze-to-investigate-churn-and-cardinality

spec.title: Slow inserts %
spec.description:
The percentage of [slow inserts](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/faq/#what-is-a-slow-insert) compared to the total ingestion rate. 

The lower the better. 

In short, slow insert is a cache miss. There are following reasons for slow inserts to go up: 
* Ingestion of completely new, not seen before time series;
* Not enough memory to maintain big enough caches for the current workload.

If percentage remains high (>10%) during extended periods of time, then it is likely more RAM is needed for optimal handling of the current number of [active time series](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/faq/#what-is-an-active-time-series). 

See [this issue](https://github.com/VictoriaMetrics/VictoriaMetrics/issues/3976#issuecomment-1476883183) for details.

spec.title: Storage full ETA ($instance)

spec.expr:
(vm_free_disk_space_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}-vm_free_disk_space_limit_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}) 
/ 
ignoring(path) (
    (rate(vm_rows_added_to_storage_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[1d]) - 
        sum(rate(vm_deduplicated_samples_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[1d])) without(type)) * 
    (
        sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type!~"indexdb.*"}) without(type) /
        sum(vm_rows{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type!~"indexdb.*"}) without(type)
    )
    +
    rate(vm_new_timeseries_created_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[1d]) * 
    (
        sum(vm_data_size_bytes{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type="indexdb/file"}) without(type) /
        sum(vm_rows{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", type="indexdb/file"}) without(type)
    )
) > 0

spec.title: Disk writes/reads ($instance)
spec.description: Shows the number of bytes read/write from the storage layer.

spec.title: Available CPU
spec.description: Total number of available CPUs for VM process

spec.title: Available memory
spec.description: Total size of available memory for VM process

spec.title: Disk space usage
spec.description: Total amount of used disk space

spec.title: Bytes per point
spec.description: Average disk usage per datapoint.

spec.title: Cache usage % by type
spec.description:
Shows the percentage of used cache size from the allowed size by type. 
Values close to 100% show the maximum potential utilization.
Values close to 0% show that cache is underutilized.

spec.title: Series read per query
spec.description: 99th percentile of number of series read per query.

spec.title: VictoriaMetrics - single-node
